name: Moveworks
description: Moveworks is an enterprise AI platform for cross-department employee support and workflow automation. Features Agent Studio (no-code builder), Creator Studio (API extensions), and Reasoning Engine (multi-step orchestration). 100+ native enterprise integrations. Acquired by ServiceNow for ~$1.5B in late 2024. 500+ enterprise customers.
oneLiner: Enterprise agentic AI platform for cross-department employee support and workflow automation, now part of ServiceNow.
funding: Acquired by ServiceNow (~$1.5B, Nov 2024). Previously raised $305M total.
pricing: Enterprise-only, per-employee/month (~$3-8/employee). No self-serve or free tier.
capabilities: Natural language IT service desk automation
Cross-department support (IT, HR, Finance, Facilities, Legal)
Conversational AI via Slack, Teams, email, web

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Agent Studio visual no-code builder: select templates or start from scratch, define triggers, configure connected systems, set up flows, establish approval/escalation rules. Creator Studio extends with API-first for custom integrations. Targets IT admins and operations, not developers.
strengths: 100+ native enterprise connectors with deep read/write
60%+ autonomous IT ticket resolution at scale
NL interface via Slack/Teams — meets employees where they work
ServiceNow acquisition provides unmatched stability and distribution
Purpose-built for enterprise support with strong out-of-box value
weaknesses: Agent Studio is config tool more than visual programming
Closed ecosystem, no MCP, limited open standards
Pricing opacity — enterprise-only, no self-serve evaluation
Weak multi-agent — routing not true collaboration
Developer experience secondary — no Git workflows, limited debugging
notableFeatures: MoveLM — proprietary LLM fine-tuned on hundreds of millions of enterprise interactions
Agent Studio no-code builder with templates
Creator Studio API platform for custom workflows
Reasoning Engine for multi-step agentic orchestration

recentDevelopments: 2024 Q3: Launched Agent Studio no-code agent builder
2024 Q3: Reasoning Engine for multi-step agentic orchestration
2024 Q2: Creator Studio for API-based custom workflows
2025 Q1: Integrated with ServiceNow Now Platform
2025: Forrester Leader in Conversational AI for Employee Services

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Agent Studio no-code visual builder. NL descriptions scaffold logic. Templates. Under 30 min for templated agents.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Conversation analytics dashboard. Basic simulated testing. No advanced trace or regression testing.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Setup guides, API refs, Moveworks Academy. More sales-oriented than developer-oriented.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Agent Studio config changes without redeployment. Creator Studio needs more effort.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Multi-skill routing across IT/HR/Finance. Reasoning Engine sequential. Not complex topologies.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Conversational handoff with context. Manager approval. No Board Operator-style dashboards.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Agent Studio team admin. No Git-native, collaborative editing, or CI/CD for agents.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Pre-built templates for IT, HR, facilities. Curated by Moveworks, not community marketplace.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: 100+ native: ServiceNow, Jira, Okta, Workday, SAP, Salesforce, Slack, Teams. Deep read/write.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: REST API for Creator Studio. Webhooks. No MCP, GraphQL, gRPC. Proprietary framework.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: Enterprise OAuth, SAML, SSO. Per-agent role-based permissions. Credential management.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Enterprise KB crawling. Semantic index. Handles common doc formats. Purpose-built enterprise RAG.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Agents pausable/disableable. Guardrails prevent out-of-scope. Input/output filtering. Topic-level control.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: Approval workflows for provisioning/access. Manager routing. Functional, not sophisticated.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: SOC 2 Type II. Comprehensive logs. GDPR data residency. HIPAA-ready. Queryable and exportable.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Role-based admin. Team hierarchy. SSO. Per-agent permission scoping by org structure.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: SaaS-delivered. Moveworks manages infra. Limited self-hosted. ServiceNow may expand.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Enterprise-scale: 50K+ employee companies, thousands of daily requests. Millions of interactions.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Performance insights: resolution rates, response times, satisfaction, topic trends.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: 500+ enterprise customers: Broadcom, Hearst, Palo Alto Networks, Databricks. Gartner/Forrester recognized.

scoringRationale: Moveworks scores 67/100. Integration (16) leads with unmatched connector breadth. Governance (15) solid enterprise compliance. Builder (14), Deployment (14), Ecosystem (14) are solid enterprise-grade. Collaboration (10) weakest — multi-agent nascent, team dev underdeveloped.
targetUser: IT and HR teams automating employee support at scale

communityBuzz: ServiceNow acquisition (.5B) seen as validation of enterprise agent category
60%+ autonomous IT ticket resolution rate frequently cited as benchmark
Post-acquisition roadmap uncertainty — will Moveworks maintain identity under ServiceNow?
Pricing opacity criticized — enterprise-only with no self-serve evaluation path

metrics.value: Leader in Conversational AI for Employee Services

walkthrough.title: How Moveworks Works: Autonomous IT Ticket Resolution

steps.title: Employee Submits Request via Slack or Teams
steps.description: An employee types a natural language request in Slack or Teams — 'I need access to Salesforce' or 'My VPN isn't working.' Moveworks intercepts the message and begins processing.

steps.title: Reasoning Engine Classifies and Routes
steps.description: The Reasoning Engine uses MoveLM (fine-tuned on hundreds of millions of enterprise interactions) to classify the request, determine intent, and identify which enterprise systems are needed to fulfill it.

steps.title: Agent Executes Across Enterprise Systems
steps.description: The agent connects to 100+ enterprise systems (ServiceNow, Okta, Workday, Jira) to execute the request. For access provisioning, it checks role permissions, creates the ticket, provisions the license, and confirms back to the employee.

steps.title: Escalation and Human Handoff
steps.description: When confidence is below threshold or manager approval is required, the agent routes to the right human with full context. The human sees the agent's reasoning, proposed action, and supporting evidence.

steps.title: Analytics and Continuous Learning
steps.description: Employee Experience Insights dashboard tracks resolution rates (60%+ autonomous), response times, satisfaction scores, and topic trends. Each interaction improves the model for future requests.
